title: JWST/NIRSpec Micro-Shutter Throughput Loss Sandbox
question: How do target-centering error, wavelength-dependent PSF width and shutter operability alter simplified NIRSpec MSA throughput?
gap: An instrument-physics QA sandbox; not a replacement for the JWST pipeline or official path-loss corrections.
dataMode: official instrument parameters + synthetic Monte Carlo
novelty: An instrument-physics QA sandbox built from verified public NIRSpec instrument parameters; not a replacement for the JWST pipeline or official path-loss corrections, and not a novel scientific claim beyond this bounded model.
validationContract: throughput bounds (clipped to [0,1], checked every trial)
injection-recovery of a known effective centering sigma
methodology: Each Monte Carlo trial draws an independent 2D target-centering offset (Gaussian, fiducial sigma=20 mas, the verified MSA target-acquisition in-flight accuracy), a wavelength uniform on the verified 0.6-5.3 micron NIRSpec range, and a shutter-open Bernoulli draw at the verified 82.5% in-flight operability fraction. The PSF is modelled as a circularly-symmetric 2D Gaussian whose sigma follows the diffraction-limited lambda/D scaling law (D=6.5 m), calibrated to the verified 80 mas FWHM anchor at 2.5 micron. Geometric slit transmission through the rectangular shutter aperture is the exact product of two independent 1D Gaussian-CDF integrals; shutters that failed closed contribute zero throughput. Deterministic diagnostic sweeps (throughput vs offset; throughput vs wavelength) and an illustrative shutter-operability heatmap accompany the main trial ensemble.
assumptions: PSF modelled as a circularly-symmetric 2D Gaussian, not the true NIRSpec Airy/diffraction pattern with wings.
Pure diffraction-limited lambda/D PSF scaling applied across the full 0.6-5.3 micron NIRSpec range, though the literature anchor only confirms Strehl>0.8 above 2.46 micron (TODO_VERIFY).
Shutter failures modelled as independent per-shutter Bernoulli draws at the verified aggregate 82.5% operability fraction; the true spatial failure pattern is not reproduced (TODO_VERIFY).
Field-averaged shutter pitch/open-area values used; true values vary ~1-4% across the field of view due to optical distortion.
limitations: An instrument-physics QA sandbox; not a replacement for the JWST pipeline or official path-loss corrections.
Models only geometric slit-loss throughput; does not include detector QE, optical-train reflectivity or grating efficiency.
All output is synthetic Monte Carlo data built from verified real instrument parameters, never real NIRSpec telemetry.

JDox (jwst-docs.stsci.edu) pages returned only client-rendered navigation shells to WebFetch; numeric parameters instead trace to verified peer-reviewed arXiv/A&A papers and the public NASA JWST spacecraft-overview page.

citation.author: Biswajit Jana
